text.title: Advanced Type Patterns
text.summary: Generic[T] with bound=, Protocol for structural contracts, TypedDict for dict payloads, TypeGuard for narrowing, and Callable types.

rule.statement: Always add bound= to TypeVar when the generic class has meaningful constraints. TypeVar without bound accepts any type and provides no protection.
rule.rationale: Repository[int] and Repository[str] are the same class object at runtime. bound=Model restricts usage and enables useful type narrowing in the body.

rule.statement: Use Protocol instead of ABC when you do not control all implementers. Add @runtime_checkable only if you actually call isinstance(x, Proto) at runtime.
rule.rationale: Protocol avoids ABC inheritance coupling. Without @runtime_checkable, isinstance raises TypeError at runtime even though type-checking passes silently.

example.code:
from typing import TypedDict, NotRequired
class UserData(TypedDict): id: int email: str name: str is_active: NotRequired[bool] # optional field
def process_user(data: UserData) -> None: print(data["email"]) # type-safe: required field
example.note: Use TypedDict for cache payloads and queue messages; use Pydantic for HTTP bodies. Avoid maintaining both for the same data shape.
